column3: Explanation (Notes: The absolute coordinate system is a world-fixed coordinate system with a fixed origin. All relative positions are defined as (neighbor/obstacle position _ ego pedestrian position). All relative velocities are defined as (neighbor velocity _ ego pedestrian velocity). The relative coordinate system is a pedestrian-centered coordinate system, where the y-axis is aligned with the direction from the pedestrianˇŻs current position toward the destination, and the x-axis denotes the lateral direction.)
